summary: async-pipeline-builder ships system-info exfiltration trap module
details: The package's lib/trap-core.js bundles host-reconnaissance and outbound HTTP POST behavior in a single module: it requires os, https, fs, and child_process; reads os.hostname() and os.platform(); references ping and curl; assembles JSON payloads keyed by `hostname:` (lines 393, 411, 553, 600, 1023); and performs multiple POST calls (lines 385, 411, 466, 548, 549). The combination of host fingerprint collection (hostname/platform), child_process execution, and repeated POSTs from the same module matches the active-exfiltration shape rather than any documented benign use, and the file name (`trap-core.js`) is consistent with intentional victim-tracking infrastructure rather than a build/runtime helper. Installing or loading this package risks leaking installer host data and executing attacker-influenced shell commands.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
